blocks:
Ayah

يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱)
Whatever there is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah. To Him belongs all sovereignty and to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things. (1)

Key Points

Earthly rulers commit transgressions to gain and maintain their power, but Allah is a Sovereign Whom all existence sanctifies and purifies, and no injustice issues from Him. Praise (Hamd) and glorification are the right of the One Who is far removed from fault and deficiency. Therefore, the glorification (Tasbih) of Allah is mentioned first, followed by His praise. In the prayer (Salat) too, Tasbih and Hamd appear together in the bowing (Ruku') and prostration (Sujud).

Messages

1- All beings in existence possess a type of consciousness, and according to that consciousness, they are engaged in the glorification of Allah. (It is regrettable that some humans, due to abandoning worship and glorification, are a discordant patch in existence). «یسبح للّه ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض»

2- The sovereignty of Allah is absolute and has no limitations. «له الملك» (Divine sovereignty is real, not metaphorical, not borrowed, not conventional, and not temporary.)

3- In the world, many individuals who possess power and governance are subject to criticism, but Allah possesses both power and sovereignty, and He is also the object of praise. «له الملك و له الحمد»

4- He is the source of all perfections, and therefore, all praises belong to Him. «له الحمد»

5- In the world, many rulers are incapable of solving problems and implementing reforms, but Allah is a Sovereign Who is capable of doing everything. «له الملك... و هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

6- The power of Allah is uniform regarding all existence. (Creating a single stone is the same for Him as creating a chain of mountains, just as for our eyes, seeing a nail and seeing a mountain are the same.) «و هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah At-Taghabun. This Surah has eighteen verses and was revealed in Medina. Due to the ninth verse, in which the Day of Resurrection is counted as the Day of "Taghabun," this Surah has been named At-Taghabun. Taghabun means to cause another to suffer loss (to be cheated) and to draw profit for oneself. On the Day of Resurrection, everyone will be concerned with saving himself and holding others responsible. The verses of this Surah, in terms of structure and style, resemble Surah Al-Hadid and are, as it were, a summary of it. The Surah begins by stating the Oneness (Tawhid) of Allah and continues with a warning to people to monitor their deeds and pay attention to the Day of Resurrection. It concludes by advising people regarding spending (Infaq) in the way of Allah and avoiding attachment to the world.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ فَمِنكُمْ كَافِرٌ وَمِنكُم مُّؤْمِنٌ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۲)
It is He who created you. Then some of you are faithless and some of you are faithful, and Allah watches what you do. (2)

Key Points

If a person sees some dirt in the street, he asks: Who dumped this dirt? If this dirt is leveled and designed, he asks who created this design. If the designed dirt, combined with wisdom and craftsmanship, turns into a tile, he asks about the characteristics and artistry of the designer. Allah created man: «خلقكم» (He created you), but it was a most wondrous creation, due to which He referred to Himself with the phrase, «تبارك اللّه احسن الخالقین» (Blessed is Allah, the Best of creators), because all kinds of artistry, subtlety, and design have been employed in it. Just as He says in another Ayah: «لقد خلقنا الانسان فى احسن تقویم»(1) (We certainly created man in the best of stature). Yes, the creation is a sign of the Creator, and the more scholarly and wise artistry is applied to it, the greater the sign of the knowledge, wisdom, and power of that Creator. The previous Ayah referred to the glorification (Tasbih) of all beings, and this Ayah refers to the disbelief (Kufr) and ingratitude of some humans, so that the disbelievers do not imagine that Allah is in need of their worship or that their disbelief causes any harm to Allah. The previous Ayah referred to the glorification of all beings, which in a way indicated that they possess a kind of consciousness, and this Ayah refers to the distinction of man over other beings, which is the possession of will and choice. With this distinguished divine gift, man can follow the same path that the rest of creation follows, or he can go against it. Perhaps the Ayah is a form of criticism of man, implying that despite Allah's greater attention and your status as the crown jewel of creation, you swim against the current and turn toward ingratitude for blessings and thanklessness toward Allah.

1) At-Tin, 4.

Messages

1- The creation of man is a manifestation of Allah's power. «هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر - هو الّذى خلقكم»

2- Man is a being with choice and selection, not compelled. «فمنكم كافر و منكم مؤمن»

3- Man's action expresses his belief. (Instead of saying: Allah is aware of your disbelief and faith, He says: Allah is aware of your performance/actions.) «واللّه بما تعملون بصیر»

Ayah

خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَصَوَّرَكُمْ فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۳)
He created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom, and He formed you and perfected your forms, and toward Him is your destination. (3)

Key Points

The meaning of the beauty of form, "فاحسن صوركم" (He perfected your forms), is that Allah equipped man with things that help him on the path to achieving his goal. Each human body part is placed in its appropriate position, and their composition and proportion have resulted in the beauty of the human physique, especially the face. Eyelashes, eyelids, and eyebrows, which are responsible for protecting the eye, have bestowed a special beauty upon the human face. The lips, which are the gatekeepers of the mouth and the guardians of the tongue and teeth, open and close only when eating or speaking, and only to the extent necessary. In childhood, when the infant's life depends on mother's milk, they perform the function of sucking, and in adulthood, they express emotional feelings through kissing. Allah created His supreme creation, man, from insignificant water, "ماء مَهین" (2), and in a dark and obscure workshop, "فى الارحام" (3), and placed upon it the best and most beautiful form. "فاحسن صوركم" (He perfected your forms).

Differences between Allah's creations and human productions:

  1. Human productions are limited and occur in certain fields, but Allah creates everything. "خلق كلّ شى ء" (4)
  2. Human productions are sometimes futile, but Allah has created everything with a purpose. "خلق اللّه السموات و الارض بالحق" (5)
  3. There is excess and deficiency in human productions, but Allah's creation is measured. "انا كل شى ء خلقناه بقدر" (6)
  4. The producer usually has no supervision over the product after production, but Allah is never heedless of His creatures. "و ما كنا عن الخلق غافلین" (7)
  5. Man sometimes becomes tired and weary of production, but Allah never tires of creation. "ما مسّنا من لغوب" (8)
  6. Allah has guaranteed the survival of beings through pairing (duality). "و من كل شى ءٍ خلقنا زوجین" (9)
  7. God guides all His creations ontologically (by inherent nature). "اعطى كل شى ء خلقه ثم هدى" (10)
  8. All creations ultimately return to Him. "خلق السموات و الارض... الیه المصیر" (11)

All of Allah's works are the best:

His creation is the best: "فتبارك اللّه احسن الخالقین" (12) His book, the Quran, is the best: "نزّل احسن الحدیث" (13) His stories are the best: "احسن القصص" (14) His Names are the best: "وللّه الاسماء الحسنى" (15) His rewards are the best: "فله جزاء الحسنى" (16) His promises are the best promises: "وعداللّه الحسنى" (17) His judgment is the best judgment: "و من احسن من اللّه حكماً" (18)

Allah has also asked us to choose the best in all our affairs. For example:

In speech, we should say the best word: "یقولوا الّتى هى احسن" (19) In action, we should perform the best: "احسن عملاً" (20) In interaction, we should have the best behavior: "ادفع باللتى هى احسن" (21) In argument and discussion, we should choose the best method: "جادلهم بالتى هى احسن" (22) In gratitude and thanks, we should use the best method: "فحیّوا باحسن" (23) Among what we hear, we should choose and act upon the best: "یستمعون القول فیتبعون احسنة" (24) In managing the orphan's property, we should act with the best method: "لا تقربوا مال الیتیم الاّ باللتى هى احسن" (25) In motivation, we should give our actions a divine color with the best intention, which is sincerity and the intention of seeking closeness (to Him): "و من احسن من اللّه صبغة" (26)

2) المرسلات، 20. 3) آل عمران، 6. 4) انعام، 101. 5) جاثیه، 22؛ عنكبوت، 44؛ روم، 8. 6) قمر، 49. 7) مؤمنون، 17. 8) ق، 38. 9) ذاریات، 49. 10) طه، 50. 11) تغابن، 3. 12) مؤمنون، 14. 13) زمر، 23. 14) یوسف، 3. 15) اعراف، 180. 16) كهف، 88. 17) حدید، 10. 18) مائده، 50. 19) اسراء، 53. 20) كهف، 7. 21) فصلت، 34. 22) نحل، 125. 23) نساء، 86. 24) زمر، 18. 25) اسراء، 34. 26) بقره، 138.

Messages

  1. The creation of the heavens and the earth was based on a plan and purpose. "خلق السموات و الارض بالحق"
  2. Allah has attributed the forming of man's shape to Himself. "صوّركم"
  3. Man is more beautiful and perfect compared to other creatures. "فاحسن صوركم"
  4. The universe of creation is moving toward a divine destination. "الیه المصیر"
  5. The origin and the return (Ma'ad) of existence is Allah. "خلق... الیه المصیر"
  6. The necessity of creation being based on truth and purpose is the existence of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and the return of humans to Allah for recompense and reward. "خلق... بالحق... و الیه المصیر"

Ayah

يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تُسِرُّونَ وَمَا تُعْلِنُونَ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (٤)
He knows whatever there is in the heavens and the earth, and He knows whatever you hide and whatever you disclose, and Allah knows best what is in your breasts. (4)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the knowledge of Allah is mentioned three times: knowledge of all existing beings "ما فى السموات و الارض" (what is in the heavens and the earth), knowledge of the manifest and hidden aspects of humans "ما تسرّون و ما تعلنون" (what you conceal and what you declare), and knowledge of thoughts and intentions "ذات الصدور" (what is within the breasts). This is continuous knowledge, which is indicated by the form of the present tense verb "یعلم" (He knows), and profound knowledge, which is indicated by the form "علیم" (All-Knowing). Belief that Allah is watching over our deeds and knows everything is the best means for avoiding sin and maintaining piety (Taqwa). Considering that the previous Ayah stated: "و الیه المصیر" (and to Him is the return)—that the return of all is to Him—this Ayah serves as an answer to a presumed question: How will humans, who decay, disintegrate, and scatter after death, return to Allah? The Ayah answers: Allah is aware of all the disintegrated and scattered particles, and the mixing of the components of the dead does not cause them to be lost or destroyed.

Messages

1- Allah's knowledge is not limited to time, place, or specific objects or matters. He knows both what is in the heavens and the earth, and He is aware of what is done secretly or openly. «یعلم ما فى السموات... یعلم ما تسرّون...»

2- Man is not left unrestrained; rather, he is under the supervision of Allah. «یعلم ما تسرّون و ما تعلنون»

3- The hidden and the manifest are the same for Allah. «تسرّون... تعلنون»

4- Allah is aware both of man's secret deeds and of the thoughts, motivations, and secrets hidden within his breast. «تسرّون... ذات الصدور»

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَبَؤُا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ فَذَاقُوا۟ وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٥)
Has there not come to you the account of those who were faithless before? They tasted the evil consequence of their conduct, and there is a painful punishment for them. (5)

Key Points

"Wabāl" (وبال) means heaviness or burden. Food that burdens the stomach is called "Wabīl" (وبیل), and heavy rain is called "Wābil" (وابل). Subsequently, any heavy matter that causes harm or loss is called "Wabāl." This word appears four times (27) in the Quran, and in all instances, it refers to worldly affliction and hardship. Divine punishment is not exclusive to the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). In some cases, Allah punishes both in this world and in the Hereafter, such as the people of Noah who were drowned, the people of Hud who were destroyed by wind, the people of Salih who were destroyed by a terrifying cry and sound, the people of Lot who were pelted with stones, and the Companions of the Elephant (Aṣḥāb al-Fīl) who tasted the punishment for their deeds in this world through Sijjīl (baked clay) and fiery stones.

27) Al-Ma'idah, 95; Al-Hashr, 15; At-Talaq, 9; At-Taghabun, 5.

Messages

1- The people of the Prophet's era were aware of the news concerning the destroyed nations of the past. «أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ»

2- Studying the history of previous nations is recommended in Islam, and whoever does not learn lessons from history is worthy of blame and reproach. «أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَبَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا»

3- What is necessary to know is important and instructive news, not every useless piece of information. «نَبَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا» (Naba' (نبأ) refers to important news).

4- Allah is Forbearing (Ḥalīm), but some deeds are so wrongful that they result in immediate punishment. (The letter fā' (ف) indicates that not much time elapsed). «فَذَاقُوا...»

5- The reason for misfortunes is the behavior of man himself. «وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِمْ»

6- Divine punishment in this world is only to the extent of "tasting," while the complete punishment is reserved for the Day of Judgment. «فَذَاقُوا وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِمْ - وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُۥ كَانَت تَّأْتِيهِمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَقَالُوٓا۟ أَبَشَرٌ يَهْدُونَنَا فَكَفَرُوا۟ وَتَوَلَّوا۟ ۚ وَّٱسْتَغْنَى ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌ (٦)
That was because their apostles would bring them manifest proofs, but they said, ‘Will humans be our guides?!’ So they disbelieved and turned away, and Allah had no need [of their faith] and Allah is all-sufficient, all-laudable. (6)

Key Points

Propagation has various types, and the best type is practical propagation (propagation through action). Allah made the prophets from the human race so that they might taste all the bitterness and sweetness of life just like the people, and be their guides in action. However, some short-sighted people considered being human a flaw and said: "أ بشر یهدوننا" (Should a human like us guide us?!)

Istaghna (استغنى) is derived from the root ghina (غنى), meaning self-sufficiency or independence. This matter is appropriate and rightful for Allah. "و استغنى اللّه و اللّه غنىّ" (And Allah is Self-Sufficient, and Allah is Rich/Self-Sufficient). But for a human being who is need incarnate, the worst trait is considering himself self-sufficient, as the Quran has pointed to this in three instances: "امّا من استغنى فانت له تصدّى"(28) (As for him who considers himself self-sufficient, do you attend to him?) "امّا من بخل و استغنى و كذّب بالحُسنى فسنیسّره للعسرى"(29), (But as for him who is stingy and seeks self-sufficiency and denies the best reward, We will soon ease his way to difficulty.) "انّ الانسان لیطغى أن راهُ استغنى "(30), (Indeed, man transgresses when he sees himself self-sufficient.)

Spreading doubts and creating skepticism among the believers is the method of the arrogant disbelievers. In this Ayah, they say: Should a human like us be our guide? "أبشر یهدوننا"; elsewhere they said: How do you know that Salih is a messenger from God?! "أتعلمون انّ صالحاً مرسل من ربه"(31); and in another instance they said: How can a poor man become a commander and ruler?! "انّى یكون له الملك"(32)

The disbelievers have no proof; they only find it improbable (or express astonishment). Regarding prophethood, they say: How can a human guide human society? "أبشرٌ یهدوننا" Regarding the Resurrection (Ma'ad), they also say: How will we be brought back to life after our bodies have decayed and vanished into the earth? "ءاذا ضللنا فى الارض ءانّا لفى خلق جدید"(33)

Allah has no need for the faith or attention of the people. "فكفروا و تولّوا و استغنى اللّه" As He says elsewhere: "ان تكفروا انتم و مَن فى الارض جمیعاً فانّ اللّه لغنىّ حمید"(34) (If you disbelieve, you and all those on earth entirely—indeed, Allah is Self-Sufficient and Praiseworthy.) Yes, if all people build their houses facing the sun or turning their backs to it, it has no effect on the sun. And in another Ayah we read: "و من كفر فان اللّه غنىّ عن العالمین"(35) (And whoever disbelieves—then indeed, Allah is Self-Sufficient above the worlds/creation.)

(28) Abasa, 5 & 6. (29) Al-Layl, 8 - 10. (30) Al-Alaq, 6 - 7. (31) Al-A'raf, 75. (32) Al-Baqarah, 247. (33) As-Sajdah, 10. (34) Ibrahim, 8. (35) Aal-i-Imran, 97.

Messages

  1. Analyze and examine the history of past generations. "فذاقوا وبال امرهم - ذلك بانه كانت..."
  2. Divine wrath follows the completion of the argument (or the conclusive proof). "عذاب الیم - ذلك بانه كانت تأتیهم رسلهم"
  3. Throughout history, Allah has continuously sent multiple prophets for the guidance of mankind. "كانت تأتیهم" (Kānat indicates continuity in the past).
  4. All prophets possessed clear proofs and miracles. "تأتیهم رسلهم بالبینات"
  5. The disbelievers created doubt and skepticism regarding the truthfulness of the prophets without reflecting upon their proofs. "فقالوا..." (The letter fā' indicates a lack of reflection.)
  6. From the perspective of the disbelievers, being human is a weakness for the prophets. "فقالوا أبشرٌ یهدوننا"
  7. Allah is Self-Sufficient in His Essence, and Praiseworthy (Hamīd) in His actions. "غنى حمید"

Ayah

زَعَمَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَن لَّن يُبْعَثُوا۟ ۚ قُلْ بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّى لَتُبْعَثُنَّ ثُمَّ لَتُنَبَّؤُنَّ بِمَا عَمِلْتُمْ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ (۷)
The faithless claim that they will not be resurrected. Say, ‘Yes, by my Lord, you will surely be resurrected; then you will surely be informed of what you did, and that is easy for Allah.’ (7)

Key Points

Zacm (زعم) refers to a suspicion or assumption that is baseless and without proof, and wherever it appears in the Quran, it is accompanied by a form of criticism. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The word zacm in the entire Quran means falsehood. "اما علمتَ ان كلّ زعم فى القرآن كذب" (36)

In the second Ayah of this Surah, a group denied Allah. "خلقكم فمنكم كافر" In the sixth Ayah, they denied prophethood. "أ بشر یهدوننا فكفروا" And in this Ayah, they deny the Resurrection (Ma'ad). "زعم الّذین كفروا ان لن یبعثوا"

In response to the denial of the Resurrection by the disbelievers, the Quran has repeatedly affirmed its occurrence through oaths, just as He says in this Ayah: "بلى و ربّى لتبعثنّ" and in another place He says: "بلى و ربّى لتأتینّكم" (37) and in response to their doubtful question He says: "اى و ربّى انّه لحقّ" (38)

It is interesting that the Quran uses the very same words in response to the disbelievers that they themselves used. Where they ask: "اَحَقّ" (Is it true?), He says: "انّه لحقّ" (Indeed, it is true). Where they say: "لا تأتینا السّاعة" (The Hour will not come to us), He says: "لتأتینكم" (It will surely come to you). And where they say: "لن یبعثوا" (They will never be resurrected), He says: "لتبعثنّ" (You will surely be resurrected).

The proof that resurrecting the dead and informing people of their deeds is easy for Allah, "على اللّه یسیر" (is easy for Allah), lies in the attributes mentioned for Him from the beginning of this Surah until now: "له الملك" (To Him belongs the dominion), "خلقكم" (He created you), "خلق السموات و الارض" (He created the heavens and the earth), "صوركم فاحسن صوركم" (He formed you and perfected your forms), "یعلم ما فى السموات و الارض - یعلم ما تسرون و ما تعلنون - و اللّه علیم بذات الصدور" (He knows whatever is in the heavens and the earth—He knows what you conceal and what you declare—and Allah is Knower of that within the breasts).

As has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran, including: "هو الّذى یبدؤا الخلق ثم یعیده و هو اهون علیه" (39) He is the One who originates creation, then repeats it, and that is easier for Him. "كما بدءَكم تعودون" (40) Just as He originated you, you will return.

36) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 37) Saba', 3. 38) Yunus, 53. 39) Rum, 27. 40) A'raf, 29.

Messages

1- Although the disbelievers lack argumentation and speak based on mere imagination and assumption, they proclaim their slogans firmly. "زعم... لن یبعثوا"

2- In teaching and training, it is necessary to present the statements of others and then respond to them. "زعم... قل"

3- Swearing by the name of the Lord is permissible to remove doubt and uncertainty from others regarding matters of faith. "قل بلى و ربّى لتبعثنّ"

4- Answer doubts and uncertainties with emphasis. "لن یبعثوا... لتبعثنّ"

5- The reason for the Day of Judgment is retribution and reward. "لتنبئنّ بما عملتم"

6- The root of the disbelievers' denial and deeming it improbable is their considering the renewed life of man impossible. Therefore, the Quran states: What you consider difficult is easy for Allah. "و ذلك على اللّه یسیر"

Ayah

فَـَٔامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَٱلنُّورِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلْنَا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۸)
So have faith in Allah and His Apostle and the light, which We have sent down, and Allah is well aware of what you do. (8)

Key Points

The meaning of "the Light" (al-Nūr) in this Ayah is the Noble Qur'an. As we read at the beginning of Sūrat Ibrāhīm: «كتاب انزلناه الیك لتخرج الناس من الظلمات الى النور» (A Book We have sent down to you so that you may bring mankind out of the darknesses into the Light). (A Book We sent down to you) so that you may bring people out of the darknesses (polytheism, ignorance, and disunity) and guide them to the Light (monotheism, knowledge, and unity). In the previous Ayahs, the discussion was about disbelief (kufr) and the failure to believe in Allah, the Messenger, and the Resurrection, which afflicted previous nations with punishment. This Ayah states: Therefore, do not follow their path, but believe. In narrations, we read that the Light (al-Nūr) refers to the infallible Imams, because they are the embodied and speaking Qur'an. Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: They are the ones who illuminate the hearts of the believers. «هم الّذین ینوّرون قلوب المؤمنین»(41)

41) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Faith is valuable when it is comprehensive: belief in Allah, the Prophet, and the Divine Book. «آمنوا باللّه و رسوله و النور الّذى انزلنا»

2- Allah is Light: «اللّه نور السموات و الارض»(42) and whatever is sent down by Him is Light. «النّور الذى انزلنا»

3- Faith must be accompanied by action. «آمنوا... تعملون» 42) Nūr, 35.

Ayah

يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُكُمْ لِيَوْمِ ٱلْجَمْعِ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ ٱلتَّغَابُنِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِۦ وَيُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۹)
When He will you bring you together for the Day of Gathering, it will be a day of privation [and regret]. As for those who have faith in Allah and act righteously, He shall absolve them of their misdeeds and admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. That is the great success. (9)

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱٠)
But as for those who are faithless and deny Our signs, they will be the inmates of the Fire, to remain in it [forever], and it is an evil destination. (10)

Key Points

"Al-Taghabun" (Mutual Loss/Deception) means that losses become manifest. That is, on the Day of Resurrection, the disbelievers because they did not believe, the wicked because they lacked righteous deeds, and the believers because they could have performed more deeds—all will feel a sense of loss, regret, and deception (ghabn) in some way. Perhaps it means that on that Day, everyone will be thinking about how to shift his burden onto someone else, just as scenes are presented in the Quran where Satan says: "Do not blame me; you yourselves are the blameworthy ones," or the criminal wishes that instead of him, his children, all his relatives, or even all people would go to Hell, and he would be saved. Perhaps Taghabun means that corrupt leaders deceived (caused loss to) their followers, and the followers, through blind obedience, gave corrupt leaders the opportunity for greater corruption, thereby deceiving them (the leaders), because if they had not been obeyed, their corruption would not have spread so widely. (43)

"Yawm al-Jam'" (The Day of Gathering) is one of the names of the Resurrection. As He says in another Ayah: «و نفخ فى الصور فجمعناهم جمعاً» (And the Trumpet shall be blown, and We shall gather them all together.) (44)

Who is the deceived (maghbūn)? In narrations, specific examples of the deceived have been identified, which we will mention:

Whoever has two days alike is deceived (maghbūn). «من استوى یوماه فهو مغبون» (45)

Arrogance and vanity cause poverty in this world and lead to deception (ghabn) in the Hereafter. «المغرور فى الدنیا مسكین و فى الاخرة مغبون» (46)

The one who is deprived of the Night Prayer is deceived (maghbūn). «فان المغبون من حرم قیام الّیل» (47)

On the Day of Resurrection, 24 treasuries and stores will be opened for every day of one's life, and the hours in which a person did not perform good deeds will be seen as empty treasuries, and he will feel indescribable regret. «فیناله من الغبن و الاسف على فواتها حیث كان متمكّناً من ان یملأها حسنات ما لا یوصف» (48)

Whoever abandons struggle (Jihad) in the way of Allah is like the deceived (maghbūn). «من ترك الجهاد فى اللّه كان كالمغبون» (49)

On the Day of Resurrection, two groups stand opposite each other: the deceived group, (Yawm al-Taghabun), and the successful group. «ذلك الفوز العظیم» (That is the great success).

In education (tarbiyah), good tidings and warnings are necessary side by side. Ayah 9 gives the good tidings of eternal Paradise, and Ayah 10 warns of the danger of eternal Hell for the disbelievers.

43) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 44) Kahf, 99. 45) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 71, p. 173. 46) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 72, p. 173. 47) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 83, p. 127. 48) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 7, p. 262. 49) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 33, p. 573.

Messages

1- The Resurrection is a day to be remembered. ("Idh", "Idhā", and "Yawm" at the beginning of a sentence usually mean: "Remember the time when...") «یوم یجمعكم»

2- The path to salvation from deception (ghabn) is faith and righteous deeds. «ذلك یوم التغابن و من یؤمن باللّه و یعمل صالحاً»

3- Faith is inseparable from righteous deeds. «یؤمن... یعمل صالحاً»

4- Righteous deeds are an expiation for evil deeds. «یكفر عنه سیّئاته»

5- We will not enter Paradise until we are purified. First, sins must be erased, then one enters Paradise. «یكفّر... یدخله»

6- The gardens and rivers of Paradise are both numerous and permanent and eternal. «جنّات... الانهار خالدین فیها ابدا»

7- The greatest success is for a person to enter Paradise with faith and righteous deeds. «ذلك الفوز العظیم»

Ayah

مَآ أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُۥ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۱۱)
No affliction visits [anyone] except by Allah’s leave. Whoever has faith in Allah, He guides his heart, and Allah has knowledge of all things. (11)

Key Points

The believing human, because he has faith in the knowledge, wisdom, and mercy of Allah, knows that no calamity befalls anyone without reason, wisdom, and justice. Every calamity is either the result of the person's own actions: «ما اصابكم من مصیبة فبما كسبت ایدیكم» (50), or it is a means of trial and test for growth and perfection. «فاخذناهم بالبأساء و الضرّاء لعلّهم یتضرعون» (51)

The term idhn (اذن - permission/leave) in the Quran is used both in the sense of divine takwini (creative/cosmic) permission, such as the growth of plants in fertile lands, which occurs with Allah's permission: «والبلد الطیّب یخرج نباته باذن ربّه» (52), and in the sense of tashri'i (legislative) permission, similar to the verse: «اُذن للذین یقاتلون بانّهم ظلموا» (53), meaning that the oppressed are permitted to defend themselves.

Here, the intended meaning of idhn is that same divine takwini permission, meaning that all events are within the scope of His knowledge and will, and Allah is not unaware of anything. Allah has provided means and ways to attain good or evil, and every creature that utilizes any of these means will receive its result.

An example of the guidance of the heart, which brings peace to the afflicted believers, is mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah: «الّذین اذا اصابتهم مصیبة قالوا انّا للّه و انّا الیه راجعون اولئك علیهم صلوات من ربهم و رحمة و اولئك هم المهتدون» (54) Those who, when afflicted by a calamity, say: "Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return," they are the truly guided ones, in addition to receiving blessings (salawat) and mercy from Allah.

Faith, which is an internal matter, if it takes root in a person, causes the heart to be guided. And if a person's spirit and heart are guided, his behavior and speech are also guided. «یهد قلبه...»

50) Ash-Shura, 30. 51) Al-An'am, 42. 52) Al-A'raf, 58. 53) Al-Hajj, 39. 54) Al-Baqarah, 156.

Messages

1- Calamities occur within the scope of divine permission (idhn), not by chance or accident. Allah does not prevent the effect of events. «ما اصابكم من مصیبة الاّ باذن الله»

2- Faith is the cause of attracting special divine mercy and guidance in the face of calamities. «ما اصاب من مصیبة... و من یؤمن باللّه یهد قلبه»

3- Allah inspires the afflicted believers with the path of remedy and cure, or endurance, duty, and patience. «و من یؤمن باللّه یهد قلبه»

4- Whoever believes in the knowledge and will of Allah does not lose patience, reliance (tawakkul), or hope in the face of events. «و من یؤمن باللّه یهد قلبه»

5- The reception of divine favors is based on the step that the human being takes himself. «من یؤمن باللّه یهد قلبه»

6- Allah knows whether our faith is genuine or hypocritical. «و من یؤمن باللّه... و اللّه بكلّ شى ء علیم»

7- Calamities occur with divine permission and knowledge, and secrets are hidden within them that Allah knows. «ما اصاب من مصیبة الاّ باذن اللّه... و اللّه بكلّ شى ءٍ علیم»

8- A person's attention to the comprehensive knowledge of Allah is the cause of enduring calamities and steadfastness in the face of difficulties. «ما اصاب من مصیبة... و اللّه بكلّ شى ء علیم»

Ayah

وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَىٰ رَسُولِنَا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱۲)
Obey Allah and obey the Apostle; but if you turn away, then Our Apostle’s duty is only to communicate in clear terms. (12)

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۳)
Allah—there is no god except Him—in Allah let all the faithful put their trust. (13)

Key Points

In this Ayah, instead of saying: "اطیعوا اللّه و الرسول" (Obey Allah and the Messenger), it says: "اطیعوا اللّه و اطیعوا الرسول" (Obey Allah and obey the Messenger), meaning the word 'obey' (اطاعت) is repeated. This is because there is a difference between these two acts of obedience: obedience to Allah is in fixed commands, while obedience to the Messenger is in matters he issues as the Islamic ruler. The Prophet is infallible (معصوم), because commanding unconditional obedience to someone who is not infallible (غیر معصوم) is, in reality, commanding the commission of sin. The phrase "لا اله الا هو" can be understood both as negating the existence of any deity other than Allah, and as negating the worthiness of worship for anyone other than Him.

Messages

1- Obedience to Allah must be accompanied by obedience to the divine leader. «اطیعوا اللّه و اطیعوا الرسول»

2- Man has been created free; he has the power both to disobey and to obey. «اطیعوا اللّه... فاِن تولّیتم»

3- The preacher should not expect acceptance and adherence from all people. «فان تولّیتم...»

4- Even if some disobey, the Prophet is dependent on God, and Allah is His supporter. «رسولنا»

5- Divine Prophets do not have the right to compel people; they are only responsible for clear conveyance (of the message). «فانّما على رسولنا البلاغ المبین»

6- If a person fulfills his duty, the turning away of the people should not cause him discouragement or worry. «فان تولّیتم فانّما على رسولنا البلاغ المبین»

7- The propagation of religion must be clear and transparent. «البلاغ المبین»

8- Obedience is only worthy of the true Deity. «اطیعوا اللّه... اللّه لا اله الا هو»

9- The prerequisite of monotheism is reliance (توكّل) upon the One God. «اللّه لااله الا هو و على اللّه فلیتوكّل...»

10- The sign of true faith is reliance upon God. «و على اللّه فلیتوكّل المؤمنون»

11- Reliance upon Allah must be placed in all matters. «فلیتوكّل المؤمنون» is stated absolutely (unconditionally).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِنَّ مِنْ أَزْوَٰجِكُمْ وَأَوْلَـٰدِكُمْ عَدُوًّا لَّكُمْ فَٱحْذَرُوهُمْ ۚ وَإِن تَعْفُوا۟ وَتَصْفَحُوا۟ وَتَغْفِرُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٤)
O you who have faith! Indeed, you have enemies among your spouses and children; so beware of them. Yet if you excuse, forbear, and forgive, then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (14)

Key Points

‘Afw (forgiveness) means pardoning, Safh (overlooking) means refraining from blame, and Maghfirah (absolution) means forgetting and consigning to oblivion. These three matters ('Afw, Safh, and Maghfirah) are three steps in dealing with the mistakes of others, including one's spouse and children.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: When the Muslims of Mecca intended to migrate (Hijrah), some of their wives and children prevented them, swearing by Allah, "Do not migrate!" and some men accepted this and stayed behind. (55) Therefore, this Ayah was revealed, warning the men against accepting this demand from their wives and children, which was contrary to the command of the Messenger of Allah.

A spouse or child who prevents the performance of duty takes away a person's Hereafter and Paradise. Therefore, such spouses and children are considered enemies.

Throughout the entire Quran, the words 'Afw, Safh, and Maghfirah do not appear consecutively anywhere except in the context of family life with one's spouse and children. This means that even in cases where there is disagreement and one must be wary of them, 'Afw, Safh, and Maghfirah must still be observed.

Considering the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul), the criterion for distinguishing a friend from an enemy is whether they encourage good deeds or create obstacles against performing good deeds. Anyone who prevents another from fulfilling his duty for the sake of their own interests is his enemy.

Some of the migrants used to tell their wives and children: We are migrating, and if you regret it and migrate later, we will not receive you in Medina. Therefore, the continuation of the Ayah states: Although they prevented your migration in Mecca, now that they have joined you, forgive their past actions and do not blame them. (56)

We read a similar Ayah in Surah An-Noor, Ayah 22: «و لیعفوا و لیصفحوا الا تحبّون ان یغفر اللّه لكم و اللّه غفور رحیم» People must pardon and overlook. Do you not love that Allah should forgive you? And Allah is Forgiving, Merciful. (Yes, if we forgive others, Allah will also forgive us.)

Although pardoning (ta'fu) and refraining from blame (tasfahu) are important, completely overlooking the mistake (taghfiru) is more important. Therefore, firstly, the word taghfiru comes after the other two, indicating a higher stage than 'Afw and Safh. Secondly, at the end of the Ayah, Allah is described as Ghafur (Forgiving), even though Allah is also described by the other two attributes: «كان اللّه عفوّاً غفوراً» (57) or as we read in supplication: “Ya Karim as-Safh” (O Generous Overlooker).

In leading the community, the Quran also gives the Prophet three commands: «فاعف عنهم واستغفر لهم و شاورهم فى الامر» (58) meaning that besides pardoning and completely overlooking mistakes, consulting with the people and giving them dignity is necessary above all else.

It is narrated from the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: A time will come when a man's destruction will be at the hands of his wife and children. This is because they will mock him for his poverty, and he will resort to any means to fulfill their demands, thus being destroyed. «یسخرانه بالفقر فیركب مراكب السوء فیهلك» (59)

(55) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn. (56) Tafsir Namuneh. (57) An-Nisa, 99. (58) Aal-i Imran, 159. (59) Tafsir Maraghi.

Messages

1- The prerequisite of true faith is resistance against the emotional and unjust demands of one's spouse and children, even if it might lead to their enmity. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا انّ من ازواجكم و اولادكم عدواً لكم»

2- Only some spouses and children create obstacles, not all. «من ازواجكم»

3- One must be vigilant and not be deceived by unworthy spouses and children. «فاحذروهم»

4- Although one should not submit to the improper demands of spouses and children, maintain the relationship of pardon, overlooking, and forgiveness. «فاحذروهم و ان تعفوا و تصفحوا و تغفروا»

5- Proceed to the final stage in the path of covering mistakes: pardon, refrain from blame, and do not even recall it yourself. «تعفوا و تصفحوا و تغفروا»

6- Balance between duty and instinct is necessary in family life. Do not abandon duty, migration, or struggle (Jihad) for the sake of emotions and instincts, nor ignore family affections for the sake of fulfilling duty. «فاحذروهم و ان تعفوا و تصفحوا و تغفروا»

7- Following your pardon and forgiveness is the pardon and forgiveness of Allah. «ان تعفوا و تصفحوا و تغفروا فانّ اللّه غفور رحیم»

8- Divine forgiveness is accompanied by mercy and special favors. If you forgive others, Allah will both forgive you and bestow special favor upon you. «فانّ اللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

إِنَّمَآ أَمْوَٰلُكُمْ وَأَوْلَـٰدُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عِندَهُۥٓ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱٥)
Rather, your possessions, and children are a test, and Allah—with Him is a great reward! (15)

Key Points

Similar to this Ayah is Ayah 28 of Surah Al-Anfal: «واعلموا انّما اموالكم و اولادكم فتنة و انّ اللّه عنده اجر عظیم» Fitnah (فتنة) means both the hardships, problems, calamities, and matters that afflict a person and serve as a test, and it also means the conspiracy and corruption of enemies, which the Quran addresses to prevent it by stating: «و قاتلوهم حتى لا تكون فتنة»(60) (Fight them until there is no more corruption/discord.)

Interestingly, this very wealth and children, which are a cause for testing, are also the adornment of the world. «المال و البنون زینة الحیاة الدنیا»(61)

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Do not seek refuge with Allah from being afflicted by fitnah, for that is inevitable; rather, seek refuge with Allah from deviation and going astray by means of fitnahs. «لا یقولنّ احدكم اللّهم انّى اعوذ بك من الفتنة... فلیستعذ من مضلات الفتن» (Let none of you say, 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from fitnah...' rather, let him seek refuge from the misleading fitnahs), then the Imam recited this Ayah. (62)

If we believe that possessions and wealth are tools for testing and fitnah, we will not suffer from having little wealth, because the test will be easier.

Wealth is a cause for testing because it must be acquired through lawful means, it must be spent on lawful things, its obligatory rights must be paid, and gratitude for it must be rendered.

Children are a cause for testing because parents must exercise utmost care in choosing a spouse, in paying the lawful dowry to the spouse, in providing healthy nutrition for the child, in showing affection and supervising their education and upbringing, in their marriage, in their employment, and in their various inclinations. Passing through all these stages is a difficult test. Of course, if parents perform their duties well and pass the test, they will share in all the good deeds of their child.

While the enmity of spouses and children mentioned in the previous Ayah relates to some of them, being a means of fitnah and testing relates to all of them.

60) Baqarah, 193. 61) Kahf, 46. 62) Nahj al-Balaghah, Hikmah 93.

Messages

1- Attachment and dependence on wealth and children are grounds for slipping and deviation. Therefore, Allah warns (us). «انّما اموالكم و اولادكم فتنة»

2- Overcoming attachment to wealth and children in order to fulfill divine duties is a great deed; therefore, Allah has ordained a great reward. «اجر عظیم»

3- The greater the Tester, the more outstanding His prize; the Great God gives a great reward. «و اللّه عنده اجر عظیم»

4- Paying attention to the great divine reward is the means of salvation from dependence on wealth and children. «و اللّه عنده اجر عظیم»

Ayah

فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا ٱسْتَطَعْتُمْ وَٱسْمَعُوا۟ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ وَأَنفِقُوا۟ خَيْرًا لِّأَنفُسِكُمْ ۗ وَمَن يُوقَ شُحَّ نَفْسِهِۦ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۱٦)
So be wary of Allah, as much as you can, and listen and obey, and spend [in the way of Allah]; that is better for yourselves. Those who are saved from their own greed—it is they who are the felicitous. (16)

Key Points

Yūq means preservation and safeguarding, and shuhh means stinginess and carnal inclination. We read in a hadith that Imam Sadiq عليه السلام constantly prayed: «الّلهم قِ شحّ نفسى» (O Allah! Safeguard me from the stinginess of my soul). When they asked Him: Why do You constantly recite this supplication? The Imam recited this Ayah: «و من یوق شحّ نفسه فاولئك هم المفلحون»(63) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «من ادّى الزكاة فقد وقِىَ شحّ نفسه»(64) Whoever pays the Zakat (alms) of his wealth has been safeguarded from the stinginess of his soul.

In Ayah 102 of Surah Aal-i-Imran, we read: «اتّقوا اللّه حقّ تقاته» (Fear Allah as He should be feared), and in this Ayah, He says: «فاتّقوا اللّه ما استطعتم». What is the meaning of these two Ayahs? Answer: Perhaps the meaning of «ما استطعتم» (as much as you are able) is that you should observe piety (taqwa) in all individual, social, political, economic, and familial matters, which refers to quantity and comprehensiveness. And the meaning of «حقّ تقاته» (as He should be feared) refers to the quality of piety, meaning you should possess the highest degree of piety, which is wara' (scrupulousness).(65)

In the previous Ayah, wealth was introduced as a means of divine test. In this Ayah, the path to success in the test of wealth is introduced as spending (infaq) and avoiding stinginess.

Just as one must strive to the best of one's ability in the struggle against the soul and carnal desires: «فاتقوا اللّه ما استطعتم», similarly, one must prepare weapons and resources to the best of one's ability in the struggle against the external enemy. «و اعدّوا لهم ما استطعتم»(66)

63) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 64) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 65) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 66) Al-Anfal, 60.

Messages

1- To succeed in divine tests, utilize the leverage of piety (taqwa). «انّما اموالكم و اولادكم فتنة... فاتّقوا اللّه»

2- In Islam, there is no obligation beyond one's capacity or ability. «ما استطعتم»

3- Divine piety means listening to the truth, acting upon it, and attending to the poor. «اتقوا، اسمعوا، اطیعوا، انفقوا»

4- If you seek goodness, remove the obstacles, listen to the command of Allah, and give to the poor from your wealth. «انفقوا خیراً لانفسكم»

5- Protecting the body from dangers alone is not enough; safeguarding the soul from bad deeds and characteristics is also necessary. «یوقَ شحّ نفسه»

6- The path to salvation is spending (infaq) in the way of Allah. «و من یوق شحّ نفسه فاولئك هم المفلحون»

7- Love of wealth and stinginess are intertwined with the human soul. «شح نفسه»

Ayahs

إِن تُقْرِضُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا يُضَـٰعِفْهُ لَكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ شَكُورٌ حَلِيمٌ (۱۷)
If you lend Allah a good loan, He shall multiply it for you and forgive you, and Allah is all-appreciative, all-forbearing, (17)
عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱۸)
Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (18)

Key Points

The topic of Qardh al-Hasanah (a goodly loan) has been mentioned in several verses of the Qur'an (67), and discussions have been raised concerning it, which we refrain from presenting under this verse to avoid repetition. Allah, Who created the entire existence, asks the human being—to whom He Himself gave existence and wealth—to help others. He has stated that lending to others is lending to Allah, and He promises rewards and recompense many times over. This is for the sake of human growth, detaching oneself from the world, attending to the poor, serving people, solving their problems, breaking the idol of attachment to materialism, soaring towards God, and blossoming the spirit of generosity and compassion. Of course, lending is valuable when it is goodly (hasan), immediate, without obligation (no minnah), respectful, intended for a rightful purpose, its amount is sufficient to solve the problem, done for the sake of Allah without expectation from people, and does not lead to the pride of the lender. In the Qur'an, several things are mentioned as expiation for sins, including: repentance (tawbah), prayer (salat), struggle (jihad), and Qardh al-Hasanah.

(67) Al-Baqarah, 245; Al-Ma'idah, 12; Al-Hadid, 11 and 18; At-Taghabun, 17; Al-Muzzammil, 20.

Messages

1- Either spend (in charity) and give, or at least give a goodly loan (Qardh al-Hasanah). «انفقوا... تقرضوا»

2- Lending to God's creation is lending to Allah. «تقرضوا اللّه»

3- One way to protect the soul from stinginess is Qardh al-Hasanah. «یوق شُحّ نفسه... تقرضوا اللّه»

4- Do not humiliate the borrower, because your counterparty is Allah, and in reality, you have lent to God. «تقرضوا اللّه»

5- A goodly loan brings a goodly reward. «قرضاً حسناً یضاعفه لكم»

6- Lending and solving people's problems is a cause for Allah's forgiveness of sins. «تقرضوا اللّه... یغفر لكم»

7- To create motivation for good deeds, state their rewards and blessings. «یضاعفه لكم و یغفر لكم»

8- Allah is grateful to those who give Qardh al-Hasanah, so borrowers should also thank the lenders. «و اللّه شكور»

9- Allah is Clement (Forbearing), so if they do not give us a goodly loan, we should also be forbearing and patient. «شكور حلیم»

10- Allah's knowledge of the manifest and the hidden is equal. «عالم الغیب و الشّهادة»

11- Let us not imagine that God needs our loan. «العزیز»

12- Divine commands, encouragements, and rewards are based on wisdom. «العزیز الحكیم»
